reviews.title: Everything and more from what i expected from a smart watch
reviews.text: i spent a great deal of time researching smart watches, Garmin came up the winner.  I have dealt with their customer service on two occasions with another product and found them to be excellent which i a big reason for buying another Garmin product.

reviews.title: Poor battery life, feature rich
reviews.text:
If only the battery life was better... I have the GPS turned off and I only measure my blood oxygen level when I'm asleep yet it is nearly impossible for the watch to last a little more than 24 hours.

My next Garmin will be the one with a solar powered assist.

That being said for the negatives this watch has the features I wanted at a good price:

- Sleep quality/quantity
- Blood oxygen level
- Steps counter
- Flight of stairs counting
- Water intake recording ) manual input)
- Multiple activities recording (it can detect when I'm running, bike riding, etc.)

It has been durable and the screen is still scratch free.

If poor battery life isn't a deal breaker buy it.

reviews.title: After a year and a half...can't wait to switch, Spotify is the worst part of this watch by far
reviews.text:
I have owned this watch for a year and a half now. I use it almost daily for my walks/runs, weight workouts, and indoor bike workouts. The main reason I chose this watch in the first place was because it was one of the smaller ones available. I am 5'9 but have small wrists, and really disliked the 'utilitarian' look of larger watches. After a year and a half, I am so tired of the issues with it and ready to jump ship. If I used this watch more regularly (I only wear it for workouts - not all day), I would have switched a LONG time ago. The amount of people who have complained about the same issues for YEARS without Garmin doing anything makes me hesitant to purchase another Garmin product, especially with the Apple Watch, Samsung, and now Google buying out Fitbit and coming up with a watch.
- Spotify on this watch seriously sucks. If you check Spotify forums, you will find COUNTLESS threads of suggestions for the Spotify app on this watch that should truly be basic features at this price. Do they implement them or listen? No. Also, there is no way to contact Spotify customer service - you just get pointed towards their 'community forum' where you have people complaining of the same exact things with no fix. You can't skip forward on any track, at all. I listen to a lot of podcasts when I walk. Some are 2 hours in length while my walk is only 1 hour. This watch constantly either reverts to the very beginning of my podcast so I have to start all over (because again, you can't skip forward - so have fun listening to ads as well!) in anything, only updates 1 song out of 15 on my playlist or get the error 'sync failed', or some other random issue. Also, if you have a playlist on Spotify, you cannot choose the track you want to listen to. You have to hit the >> button to get to where you want. Really? I hope Garmin drops Spotify because they have been a truly horrible app on this watch the entire time with no effort to make updates. Take a look at the reviews of Spotify on the Garmin store if you don't believe me.
- Battery life is a couple of days. However, there are points in time where my watch will be at say 80% the night before, I get ready to leave and go my walk the next morning, pick up my watch, and it's at 8%. WTF? This happens enough to be super annoying and apparently is not an 'only me' problem. It appears to be tied to when the watch updates.
- I tried to wear this to track my sleep in the beginning and gave up after two nights due to the extreme disco effect of the 'gesture' motions. I tried turning on the 'do not disturb' function advertised on the watch which is supposed to disable this stupidly bright light that WILL wake you and whoever is next to you up. Didn't work. Do a search for 'Garmin watch lights up in middle of the night' and you will find all sorts of people complaining of the same thing, and not just on this version of the watch.
- GPS has issues connecting and can take several minutes which is pretty annoying when you try to start tracking an activity only to see a red bar when trying to connect. I 'sync' my watch to my phone constantly so that is not the issue. I have switched between the GPS options Garmin offers on the watch to no avail. Search for this issue as well and you will see lots of people with the same complaint.

reviews.title: GET THIS WATCH!!!
reviews.text: I LOVE this watch!!! It is so easy to use, tracks all of the metrics I am interested in (steps, sleep, stress, activities, hydration, menstrual cycle) and then some!! It is very accurate and the app is very user- friendly. The watch itself has worked perfectly and I have found it to be comfortable and intuitive to use.  It holds a charge for several days on end and when it does need to be charged, it reaches full battery very quickly! I looked at a lot of different watches and considered a lot of different options. I am so happy that I went with the vivoactive 4! Garmin, you all did a great job with this watch!! I honestly could not be happier with my choice!!

reviews.title: A Gift that went well
reviews.text:
This was a Christmas gift for my wife to replace her older Vivosmart. She wanted the new features but she has a small wrist.
Doing some searches I thought this would be as big as I should try.

All went well. It does all she needs and while it's much better it's not too big. Doing larger does add more battery time and more features, but this is the right fit for her

reviews.title: Just ok
reviews.text: The band fits as expected. I like the magnetic design and the magnet holds well. My problem with it is it gets caught on fabric and has pulled on some of my clothes. It also pinches sometimes.

title: Michael Kors Access Gen 4 MKGO Smartwatch- Lightweight Touchscreen Powered with Wear OS by Google with Heart Rate, GPS, NFC, and Smartphone Notifications
features: Smartwatches powered with Wear OS by Google work with iPhone and Android Phones

Heart Rate & Activity Tracking using Google Fit; Built in GPS for distance tracking; Swimproof design 3ATM; Google Assistant built in it’s your own personal Google, always ready to help; Speed through checkout with your watch using Google Pay

This is one smart watch receive smartphone notifications and app alerts, manage your calendar, control your music, download third party apps (your favorite ride sharing or music apps), and customize your watch face; touchscreen functionality, built in fitness tracker, microphone, music storage & controls, custom goal & alarm settings, calendar alerts, multiple time zones, weather, LED flashlight, wireless syncing
Estimated 24 hour battery life, based on usage plus 2 additional days in low power mode; magnetic USB rapid charger included; charge up to 80 percent in under an hour
AMOLED display: active-matrix organic light-emitting diode. Water resistant up to 50m: Wearable while swimming in shallow water

reviews.title: It could have been so much better.
reviews.text: I have so many feelings about this watch. First off, set up was a breeze. You just open your app store and get the Google app directed. Pairing and synching up your info is very easy. The watch is very beautiful! I was scared the crystals would rub right off like glitter, but I have not had any issues so far. Now for all the bad stuff.. first and foremost the battery life is terrible. I would give it 9-10 hours max. I'm not much of an app person either, so if you are then that means your battery life will be even lower. It has its own charger, so that means you'll have to carry it around with you, especially big your planning to go out after work or school which is very inconvenient. I with it was a standard C USB charger so I can just use my phone charger, but sadly that's not the case. I did an experiment to see how long the battery would last. I charged it to 100%, and left it in my desk right before bed. In the morning at 8 am, I am at 15%. Whoa. Yeah, the battery is just really really terrible. So I thought I'd just change the battery settings..well there is only one other option and that is to go into battery saving mode where all it will display is the time. That in my opinion defeats the purpose of a smart watch. Other than that, I love how I can customize watch faces by using my social media, or pick from a pre made list by Michael Kors. You can also get email notifications, download apps like Google keep to take notes by speaking into your watch, as well as use Google pay, activity tracker, weather, and pause and play music without taking out your phone. So yeah, if your okay with a short battery life, then everything else about this watch is awesome. Just giving you the real tea. Hope you found this review helpful.

reviews.title: Great feminine watch for dress up or down
reviews.text: This watch works with android and iPhone which makes this a winner in my book. It does pretty much everything my iwatch series 4 does and it looks beautiful on my wrist. Charger is magnetic and easy to use.

reviews.title: Easy set up - does what a smartphone can do
reviews.text:
My wife really likes this watch. This is the one with the glittery watchband... It comes with a magnetic charger that just attaches to the back - no fidgeting. And I was surprised at how quickly it charged.

The screen colors are great! Vibrant colors and clear graphics. The touchscreen itself can lag just a little, but overall responds very well. Neither one of us have ever had a smartwatch before and I was a little apprehensive about setting it up, but I downloaded the wear OS app to her phone and from there, it just walked us through the process. It paired on the first try and seems to be working just as it should.

She's now got it set up to check emails and can generally do anything on the internet that she would do with her phone. Then theres the added advantage of the fitness stuff... heart rate monitor, etc.

All in all, she loves it!

reviews.title: Still Searching
reviews.text:
The MKGO has the best battery life of any WearOS watches I've tried. On a fairly busy day with the app running in the background on my iPhone the whole time, I was able to wear the watch from 6 a.m. until I went to bed around 10. The other three android watches I've tried didn't make it to dinner.

But this doesn't have a speaker.

Why doesn't this have a speaker?

One of the indispensable features of my Apple watch is being able to answer a call quickly when I have stepped out of the room my phone is in. I have lupus. I can't run. People somehow seem to know to call about something important when I've gone to the bathroom or stepped out to the mailbox. I'm close enough to be connected to my phone, but not close enough to answer it.

This never happens with spam calls. Just the ones from the people I won't be able to get back to through their nightmare phone menus.

The lack of this feature makes the watch unsuitable for me, and that makes me sad, because the iPhone is a begware spamming disappointment machine these days, and I really want to move to android.

reviews.title: Nice watch
reviews.text: Shipping was very slow but the watch is nice.  Took minutes to install.  easier than my apple watch.  does everything i need that my series 4 does but is discrete and fashionable.  Love the pink color..... my favorite.  Touchscreen works fine and easy to navigate.  A great buy for those who dont necessarily want an apple watch.  I am able to sync my contacts and apps to the watch.  Tracks my heart-rate on command.  I love this watch.

You get 3 full bands, One black... one gray.. and one light aqua blue - it's more of a very light gray blue.  They are a rubbery ? silicone like? material. Smooth, no sharp edges.  They attached easily. So far, so good.  Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

reviews.title: Seems to work pretty well for the most part
reviews.text: Setup was easy with a quick app download and it paired with my iPhone 12 without any drama. Biometric tracking seems to work reasonably well with the exception of heart rate, which seems to always read 70 no matter what I'm doing. I'm not too crazy about the aesthetic of the watch, it just looks kind of cheap to me. I got this mainly to use as a health tracking tool and to tell time, not to make and receive calls/texts with, so I haven't setup any of those features yet and may never get around to doing so. So i can't comment on ease of use for any of those features. I did like that there are quite a lot of different watch face designs that can be applied and without any additional purchase. For the price of around $40, this is a pretty smokin' deal for a smart watch, I think.

reviews.title: Not bad for the price
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a few different affordable watches and my biggest issue has been the charging port. They have trouble staying connected with tiny pins and no magnetic pull. So going into this I’m more critical.
Upside is there is definitely a better charging system. It holds in place nicely even though it’s a pretty small pin area it is easier to charge.
The screen does have low sensitivity response. When pairing it it took 3 tries to get it recognize I had tapped the screen.
On a good note it’s reading well and has some great features like heart rate, oxygen, and sleep. Everything lined up well with my normal statistics so I feel it’s pretty accurate.
There’s a variety of images to choose from so you can customize the screen however you would like.
It’s very comfortable. The band holds like a premium watch band. It has a large face that is classy. Aesthetically it’s very nice.
Overall for an affordable smart watch it’s a decent option.

reviews.title: Okay tracker
reviews.text: For the price, it is an okay tracker.  The microphone on the watch is not of great quality as I was told sounded muffled on it.  It does have a lot of features and was quite easy to use but nothing about it made it stand out from the other hundreds of smart watches out on the market.

reviews.title: Cute watch is all I can say
reviews.text: Initially the watch was quick to charge and holds the charge for a couple of days.  The functions were easy to use, and a lot to choose from for the activities I do.  I mainly track my steps, distance, duration, heart rate.  It worked well for a few weeks, then I took it swimming for a few laps in the pool.  That was a mistake, battery stopped working (won't charge anymore).  Sad, I really liked the watch, it's cute but the battery died.

reviews.title: Great Smart watch for the essentials
reviews.text:
I was looking for a good smart watch for my mother. She was a Fitbit user but her Fitbit died after years of use and wanted something with a similar feel.
This watch really worked out for her.
It's got a great screen and a good amount of apps to provide essential uses.
It's able to work with iOS and Android.
It's able to get notifications from apps and make phone calls.
Not a bad product at all.

reviews.title: Very accurate on counting steps
reviews.text: I'm happy I found this! In my opinion, it is way more accurate than wearing my Fitbit on my wrist. I've gone from thinking I was a snail and not moving most days to a walking whiz! Just keep in mind if you are out in public with pants that don't cover it, folks may look at you. It looks a little like an electronic monitoring bracelet. Which is fun to me. Let them wonder what I've been up to.....

reviews.title: Heart rate not detectable most of the time
reviews.text: It's nice for working out. But it does not track heart rate with a darn. Lights facing in though the mesh tight on the skin and nadda. A big disappointment. Directly on the skin, adjusted all around. Charge 3

reviews.title: Didn't count steps
reviews.text: I used this for a few weeks because I didn't feel my band was counting steps when I was shopping or carrying something.  I was very surprised to see that after a full day out-and-about I had under 300 steps on the counter.  That's roughly what I would normally have when I got up to get my morning coffee.  I tried re-adjusting and tried the other leg, but got the same result.  I'm back to keeping my tracker on my wrist.  Disappointed.

reviews.title: Comfortable and Lightweight
reviews.text: I love this! It is exactly what I need to help me keep moving and to accurately register steps as I work to increase my activity! The band is light and very comfortable. I hardly even notice that I have it on. The band does have some stretch to it which helps in making it easy to put on.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text: This band has helped me continue to get my steps when I am watching and carrying grandchildren, pushing a stroller or grocery cart, doing a lot of carrying around the house...I love it!  It is comfortable and secure!  With four snaps I can't imagine how you could lose it!

reviews.title: Nice resolution
reviews.text:
I bought this watch because of the high-resolution display. And that's what I got, no more, no less.

Upon first impression, I can tell the display has crisp graphics. The touch screen is responsive. The processor is fast. Overall, the phone itself is nice.

The app, on the other hand, is awful. It is intuitive, but it loses Bluetooth connectivity without reason. This is the only app that I've seen that will move forward and backward during a dial sync. When I switch to certain dials, the progress bar will reach 61%, then count back down to 60%, then 59%, then start going back up. It's nice to see honesty in an app, but it's just strange.

I didn't test notifications from other apps, but no SMS text messages came through. The setup is simple and easy with SMS notifications settings on its own section. But once set up, no messages came through.

Battery life? What battery life? After a fully charge, this watch drained to 90% in the 15 minutes I used to overlook the features and set up the app. This from a 330mAh battery. At this rate, the battery will be depleted in less than two hours.

reviews.title: Junk
reviews.text: What a piece of junk. The instructions don't match the watch or the Amazon description and is written in awful broken English. And the app--- AWFUL. Navigation is bad, the English on the app is even worse than the instructions. I'm seriously concerned that downloading the app has somehow compromised anything on my phone. While I understand Amazon is a gigantic company that can't police every single item for sale, this is just BLATANTLY bad. In case my review isn't already clear, DO NOT BUY.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price
reviews.text: It was very difficult for me to set up this watch until I scanned the scan code from the pamphlet. The directions weren't easy. I think I would like a watch a little smaller but I like the shape and bands.  The price was great. I'm still not sure about the sleep mode, nothing seems to be registering. Steps are showing up which is cool, BPM is registering. Still figuring it out. Arrived promptly.  Additional info: the watch is NOT tracking my erratic sleep pattern, which I really needed it to do. I also found the size of the round watch intrusive to my long sleeve shirts and arm. Loved the pink and green bands and the color of the watch face. Steps are also not being accurately recorded. So sad, really had high hopes for this.

reviews.title: Worth the money n saved my life
reviews.text: Love the feminine color. Easy to see. Helps me keep track of certain health irregularities as they happen therefore possibly averting a health emergency. Ty for having an ingenious machine in such a lovely and compact device.

🍀【Two Sizes of HD Color Touch Screen】UFit Pro 44mm have 1.69'' large HD color full screen,UFit Pro 40mm have 1.5'' mini HD color full screen.They have the same function.and ensures smooth operations and 240*280 resolution for you.

reviews.title: I wanted to love it
reviews.text: It really isn't the watches fault that I returned it.  It did all it said it would do, the Alexa feature worked, the heart rate monitor worked , the picture was bright, among other things. I didn't like that if you use the timer ( which I use frequently at my job) you can't go back to the main screen, if you do it turns off the timer, but you can use Alexa as a timer and don't have to stay on the same screen so there is a work around. I was also dissapointed with the fact there were only 4 face choices.  But, the reason I returned it is that my phone is older and it wears down my battery quickly and was causing cpu (?) overload warnings.

reviews.title: Solid watch
reviews.text:
Watch came well packaged and was easy to set up and pair using the provided instructions on the item page. It was made well and kept good time. I have a Motorola phone and it would not stay paired with my phone. The sleep feature never seemed to work because it was constantly disconnecting from the app. It may be a good choice for someone else, but I returned it.

The battery life was excellent and it was a well constructed and comfortable watch

reviews.title: So comfortable!
reviews.text: Best band I’ve ever worn! These are so comfortable and lightweight that I sometimes forgot I have my Fitbit on! It’s breathable! I like that it was so many holes that you can use to tighten with. Sometimes with others their so far apart that it’s righter too loose or too tight. Trust me, you will not have that issue with this band! The design is so beautiful and the colors are perfect!

reviews.title: Great bands
reviews.text: Comfortable and lots of holes to change size. Looks pretty and I like the thinner band.

reviews.title: Easier to set-up than previous bands
reviews.text:
I have really struggled getting replacement bands in place in the past. I had this one switched over in a matter of minutes. The lacy look makes it less apparent that the band is vinyl and the dark colors help to hide stains. I have bad metal allergies nd was a bit afraid of what would happen with the clasp given it is metal and has some contact with my skin. Been wearing this since December and the only time I have issues with a rash was when I had the watch fastened too tightly to my wrist. I believe this was from trapped sweat and moisture and not a metal allergy reaction.
Extra great deal in that you get three bands. I don't foresee a need to buy more bands for at least a year.

reviews.title: Pretty
reviews.text: Cute style; nice colors; comfortable to wear.

reviews.title: Comfortable and affordable
reviews.text:
I really like the pink bandThe material is cool and comfortable. It is easy to change the bands for a different look.
I would definitely buy again.

title: LFUTARI Smart Watch Compatible with iPhone and Android Phones, Fitness Tracker with Heart Rate Monitor, Sleep Monitor & Blood Oxygen Saturation, 5ATM Waterproof Smartwatch for Women Men - Black
description: ※※※Please carefully read the User Manual before using it. Any questions, please feel free to contact the seller for help. Smart Phone System Requirements iOS 11.0 & higher Android 6.0 & higher Bluetooth 4.0 & below Specifications Sync: Bluetooth 5.0 Screen: 1.3" TFT LCD Battery Capacity: 210mAh Working Voltage: 5V-500mA Working Temperature: -10℃ to 40℃ Weight: 1.27oz (36g) Waterproof Rating: 5ATM Working Time: 5-7 days What's in the Box? Smartwatch with bands  Charging cable User manual Quick start guide ※※※Please note: Smart watch data cannot be used for medical purposes. The monitoring data is for reference only. If you need medical help, please contact a professional doctor.※※※
features: 【Smart Watch for Men and Women】Features a 1.3-inch TFT-LCD full touch screen with a simple and vivid interface. 5 levels of brightness are available so you can easily view the screen in sunlight, and it will auto adjust brightness at night with night mode on. The screen lights up automatically when you lift and turn your wrist. Skin-friendly and soft bands fit different wrist sizes.

【All Day Activity Tracking】Supports 14 sports including walking, running, cycling, yoga, swimming and more. Accurately record all-day activities such as steps, distance, calories burned, miles walked, active minutes. Connect your GPS on your smartphone to see your real-time pace and distance during your outdoor activities and record your workout route map in the app.

【Healthy in Every Day】the latest advanced sensor for accurately monitor your real-time heart rate, blood oxygen (SpO2) level. Auto-track analyze your sleep quality(deep sleep, light sleep and wake up time) to help you get a better understanding of your health and and make reasonable adjustment on your lifestyle.

【5ATM Waterproof & Long Battery Life】Enjoy up to 7 days extra-long battery life, and our fitness tracker watch is designed with 5ATM waterproof, which means you can wear it without worries even while swimming. This watch also has many pratical tools such as alarm clock, music controller, deep breathing guide, sedentary reminder, stopwatch, timer and more.

【Smartwatches for Android IOS Phones】Compatible with most iOS 11.0 and Android 6.0 above, Bluetooth 4.0 below smartphones. Download and install our custom app (please check page 5 of the manual for details), bind this smartwatch to your phone, you can receive alerts of calendar, incoming calls, SMS, APP notifications and SNS (Messenger, WhatsApp, Skype, Twitter, etc) and preview the messages, never let you miss anything important things (note: you can't reply to messages or make calls).

【Multiple Dials & Custom Watchface】this smartwatch itself comes with 3 default dials, you can change it via watch's setting page. Also there are 14 dials in the dial gallery in our custom app, you can go download and install them to your watch at any time, and you can also choose a photo from your phone album as the background of your watch.

reviews.title: Best gift ever
reviews.text: Bought this for a 9 year old. It’s amazing. It has so many things on it and is easy to use. She shows it to everyone who will look at it and she feels like it’s just like her big brothers and sisters watches. Very happy.

reviews.title: 2G is Dead
reviews.text: This watch says that it utilizes a sim card with 2g service. This service is nearly dead everywhere. The sim card area in the watch is a little tricky to figure out. You have to open the back and then lift up the battery slightly. The sim card slot is located under the battery. You have to slide(left/right) and lift up the metal sim card cover like a door. Insert the sim card and lay the metal cover down over it and slide it into place to lock it. This watch is not waterproof. The watch works as specified except for the 2g calling. 2g service is not available in my area, so make sure you check if your area still has it. You have to buy your own sim card and they recommend Speedtalk service. Check their coverage map for 2g service.
